# UC-23.4.3 · Diversity and Inclusion Metrics Dashboard

## Description

Rolls active-worker snapshots into normalized role ladders and gender cohorts, computes within-level representation percentages with k-anonymity cell suppression (counts under five dropped before publication), compares live percentages to staged diversity-target lookups, and surfaces Met versus Below-target gaps — so HR can spot glass-ceiling slopes (representation plunging between junior IC bands and executive tiers), adverse-impact promotion-rate erosion against four-fifths guardrails, and progress narratives tied to EEO-1 Component 1 category modeling rather than flight-risk scoring.

## Value

Executive sponsors and people-analytics leads need defensible workforce-composition visibility that aligns with US EEOC EEO-1 Component 1 filings, UK Gender Pay Gap reporting timelines, and EU Pay Transparency Directive disclosure readiness — without exporting row-level ethnicity or gender markers to spreadsheets outside Splunk’s RBAC envelope. When representation gaps widen faster than hiring can refill pipelines, boards see measurable distance from stated inclusion commitments; tying Splunk aggregates to quarterly snapshots preserves audit lineage while steering calibration for succession, sourcing spend, and promotion-committee scrutiny.

## Implementation

(1) Land HRIS demographics via DB Connect JDBC or REST modular inputs into `business`, then summarize into `hr_demographics.csv` / `diversity_targets.csv` via scheduled searches with DPIA-approved fields only. (2) Map job_grade strings consistently to ladder buckets before trending year-over-year. (3) Restrict dashboards to aggregate SPL + lookups; route alerts on sustained Below-target streaks to HR Legal–approved mailing lists — never broadcast suppressed cells.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
Dedicated Splunk roles scoped to aggregate indexes only — deny raw HRIS sourcetypes for analysts without DPIA clearance. Splunk DB Connect on a Heavy Forwarder with JDBC URLs plus credential vault entries for Snowflake, Oracle, Postgres, or SQL Server replicas backing Workday or SuccessFactors warehouses; outbound HTTPS from modular-input hosts to vendor REST endpoints (`443/tcp`) and Splunk HEC (`8088/tcp`). Legal-approved retention policies for lookups versus indexed snapshots (often ninety-day dashboard freshness with seven-year archival for regulatory attestations).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stand up nightly saved searches that dedupe worker keys (`employee_id`, `Worker_ID`, or `personIdExternal`) from `index=business` sourcetypes such as `workday:demographics`, `sf:personal`, or `dbx:hris_workers`, projecting normalized columns (`job_grade`, `gender`, `ethnicity`, `status`, `country`) before `outputlookup hr_demographics.csv`. For Path A (Workday RaaS), expose JSON via Integration System User credentials hitting `customreport2` URLs with OAuth2 or basic auth; throttle pagination per tenant guidance. For Path B (SuccessFactors OData), poll `/odata/v2/PerPersonalInformation` with selective `$select` lists per GDPR Article 9 minimization. For Path C (Oracle Fusion via REST), capture Fusion worker payloads then warehouse snapshots via DB Connect selecting Oracle-native compensation-grade joins where SOAP parity matters. Maintain `diversity_targets.csv` keyed by `role_level` plus `gender_normalized` goal percentages sourced from annual DEI council approvals.

Step 2 — Create the search and operational alerts
Paste the SPL into Search — lookups must reside under `lookups/` with Definitions referencing CSV kits. Pair aggregate alerts when `status_flag="Below target"` persists five consecutive weekly snapshots for the same ladder × cohort intersection after suppression filters — throttle keys should concatenate ladder plus cohort codes so transient spreadsheet reruns do not page executives.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare Splunk ladder totals against Workday headcount dashboards sharing identical effective dates (As-of snapshots versus rolling ingestion skew ties back to `_time`). Cross-check suppressed breakout counts (`where headcount >= 5`) with HRBP spreadsheets ensuring suppressed cohorts carry literal `<5` footnotes outside Splunk exports only after Legal release. Confirm promotion-cycle milestones align — enterprise promotions concentrated in January produce stair-step jumps unrelated to inclusion regressions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard Studio layouts combine stacked-bar cohort charts with Markdown captions documenting jurisdiction-specific reporting cadences — US EEO-1 Component 1 filing windows versus UK April disclosures versus EU Pay Transparency Directive timelines beginning June 2026 for qualifying employers. Embed links to regulator portals inside collapsed panels rather than email chains carrying identifiers.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
Self-ID refresh skew: sharp increases following annual campaigns typically stem from refreshed willingness-to-disclose rather than organic turnover — annotate dashboards with campaign windows pulled from HR communications calendars.
Regional opt-out deltas: blending US (~8%), EU (~18% post GDPR), and UK (~12%) cohorts without country segmentation corrupts YoY narratives — enforce mandatory `country` filters or normalized macro prompts.
Small-population noise: thirty-person engineering pods swing gender ratios multiple percentage points after one departure — insist on trailing-quarter smoothing before escalation.
M&A mixes: integrations adding hundreds of roles quickly inflate ladder denominators — alert when any ladder grows twenty-plus percent inside ninety days and freeze YoY comparisons until integration QA stamps mappings.
Contractor scopes: contingent-worker inclusion skews versus OFCCP FTE-only affirmative-action datasets — dual dashboards labelled explicitly.
Grade renaming churn: reorganisations renaming IC4 → Senior Engineer without historic bridging breaks longitudinal trending until SAP SuccessFactors or Workday compensation-grade histories reconcile.
UK Equality Act constraints: ethnicity prompts differ materially from US EEO categories — forbid naive overlay charts comparing uncoded cohort labels.
Pay-equity cadence mismatch: workforce snapshots refresh nightly while compensation parity analyses run semi-annual — synchronize commentary timestamps referencing payroll freeze dates.
Promotion-cycle calendars: annual promotion batches inject discrete jumps absent policy drift — differentiate stair-steps using HR calendar overlays.
Voluntary versus involuntary exits: layoffs distort demographic exit cohort mixes versus resignations — segment Splunk turnover extracts using termination-reason taxonomies.
Trans/non-binary lifecycle edits: longitudinal gender mixes shift when HR systems adopt progressive markers — archive surrogate ladder histories via Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 extracts before defending litigation-era narratives.
Schedule A disability disclosure rates hovering near five percent reflect voluntary disclosure norms rather than ingestion defects.
VEVRAA veteran self-ID percentages (~7–8%) swing after survey wording tweaks — validate vendor release notes before reacting.
Cell-suppression optics: intersections such as Asian women in C-suite often remain suppressed — mirror Legal-approved disclosure matrices noting `<5` placeholders.
Ontology divergence: US EEOC race buckets versus UK Office for National Statistics ethnic-group schemas versus Brazilian IBGE classifications require lookup bridges prior to multinational rollups.
Workday hierarchy collisions: mixing Position_Reference progression with Job_Family ladders yields duplicated ladder assignments — governance councils pick exactly one hierarchy per fiscal measurement.
Promotion_Date gaps: optional Workday custom fields missing entries force reliance on Compensation_Grade effective-dated deltas extracted via audit trails.
Glass-ceiling benchmarks: cite Women in the Workplace or LinkedIn Talent Insights only after confirming sector cohort comparability.
Four-fifths screening checks: derive promotion-rate ratios separately from representation dashboards — Splunk alerts reference HR-approved statistical tooling where Fisher tests exceed Splunk primitives.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup hr_demographics.csv
| where status="active"
| eval role_level=case(
    match(job_grade,"^IC[1-3]$"), "Individual Contributor — Junior",
    match(job_grade,"^IC[4-5]$"), "Individual Contributor — Senior",
    match(job_grade,"^M[1-2]$"), "Manager",
    match(job_grade,"^M[3-4]$") OR match(job_grade,"^Director$"), "Director",
    match(job_grade,"^VP$") OR match(job_grade,"^SVP$"), "VP",
    match(job_grade,"^C(EO|FO|TO|HRO|OO)$"), "C-Suite",
    1=1, "Other")
| eval gender_normalized=coalesce(gender, "Prefer not to say")
| eval ethnicity_normalized=coalesce(ethnicity, "Prefer not to say")
| stats dc(employee_id) as headcount by role_level, gender_normalized
| eventstats sum(headcount) as level_total by role_level
| eval representation_pct=round(100*headcount/level_total, 1)
| where headcount >= 5
| sort role_level, - representation_pct
| join type=outer role_level gender_normalized [
    | inputlookup diversity_targets.csv
    | rename target_pct as goal_pct
    | fields role_level, gender_normalized, goal_pct ]
| eval gap_vs_goal=round(representation_pct - coalesce(goal_pct, 0), 1)
| eval status_flag=case(
    isnotnull(goal_pct) AND gap_vs_goal >= 0, "Met",
    isnotnull(goal_pct) AND gap_vs_goal >= -3, "Approaching",
    isnotnull(goal_pct) AND gap_vs_goal < -3, "Below target",
    1=1, "No goal set")
| table role_level, gender_normalized, headcount, representation_pct, goal_pct, gap_vs_goal, status_flag
```

## Visualization

Stacked-bar panels for representation share by role-level × gender cohort (parallel boards swap gender for ethnicity once ontology-aligned). Line charts with shaded goal bands for quarterly trailing representation versus targets (annotate McKinsey Women in the Workplace benchmarks as optional reference overlays). Funnel or cascade visuals from Individual Contributor — Junior through C-Suite to expose pipeline thinning for women and minoritized groups. Single-value tiles with year-over-year delta on enterprise-wide representation metrics where sample sizes permit. Heatmaps crossing department × role_level when HR taxonomy joins stay stable. Sankey diagrams for hiring, promotion, and exit flows keyed by anonymized cohort codes — publish only where flows exceed suppression thresholds.
